violations.description: Each form element must have visible label defined and associated using a For attribute.
violations.help: To fix this issue, form elements must have associated visible labels. Benificial for the users who perceive and operate interfaces through text or speech based technologies.

any.message: This <input type="text"> element is missing an accessible name. Users need a label to understand what input is required. Add a <label for="..."> or use aria-label.

nodes.failureSummary: This <input type="text"> element is missing an accessible name. Users need a label to understand what input is required. Add a <label for="..."> or use aria-label.

violations.message: Each form element must have visible label defined and associated using a For attribute.

violations.description: Page at least must have one level one heading. Preferably on top of the page.
violations.help: To fix this issue, page should have one H1 styled text for better navigation. Benificial for users who perceive, operate and understand the interface via assistive technologies.

any.message: Page is missing a level-one heading. Screen reader users rely on `<h1>` for main content identification. Ensure the page has exactly one visible `<h1>`.

nodes.failureSummary: Page is missing a level-one heading. Screen reader users rely on `<h1>` for main content identification. Ensure the page has exactly one visible `<h1>`.

violations.message: Page at least must have one level one heading. Preferably on top of the page.

violations.description: Text must have sufficient contrast against its background. Low contrast text must be difficult for users with low vision to read.
violations.help: To fix this issue, text must meet the WCAG 2.1 AA contrast ratio of 4.5:1 for small text (or 3:1 for large text). This is benificial for the users having low vision and other related issues.
